notes: Three agy lifetimes back-to-back: v1 clean Ctrl-D exit after a no-op turn → settles to ready (and, single-process, should emit process_exited); v2 SIGKILL mid-idle → process_exited while ready (the load-bearing teardown observable); v3 SIGKILL mid-turn → killed while working, the short tail may end in working (the sweep-to-ready is a live-dashboard property outside the recording window). Each variant's birth is a presession proc-<PID> → conv-id session pair; later phases pin same_session_as the variant's session_birth, and each session_birth is new_session:true so a later variant can't match an earlier ready. Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL).

text: Variant 1: presession proc-<PID> row appears at launch
Variant 1: conv-id session appears in ready after the first turn
Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready (terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE, no tool_calls); the clean Ctrl-D exit leaves it in ready
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)
Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while ready; no transition to working/waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes

Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — user prompt drives ready → working, then killed mid-turn. No process_exited asserted: the kill lands while working and the 8s tail may be too short for the process-scan sweep before the recording daemon shuts down, so the recording may end with the session in working.
